feedback.text: Regarding Digital Contracts, I believe it should be required for software that relies on some form of online service to, at the end of it's service life, provide resources to the customers to either self-host the service, or provide enough documentation for the customer to be able to build the missing parts of the service themselves. This means that if a software service reaches end of life, the customer can still use the service in enough of a capacity to either continue their work, or move it to other software. When applied to video games, this means that customers can still play the parts of the games that do not require online services to function, or host their own servers for multiplayer when the official servers are no longer running.
